title: Cache should be cleaned after package installation
description: Cache should be cleaned after package installationAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellIn Docker, when packages are installed via a package manager, an index is cached locally by default. This index should either be cleaned up or stored in a dedicated cache mount.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoDocker images should only contain the necessary data. The package index is redundant for the correct operation of the installed software.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Cache should be cleaned after package installation. Package manager cache should be removed to reduce Docker image size. Use 'apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' for apt, 'yum clean all' for yum, 'dnf clean all' for dnf, or '--no-cache' flag for apk.
